title: Very slow and redundant work: Copying blob ... skipped: already exists  (skopeo 1.9.3)
body:
I'm using `skopeo copy` but it's very slow (5-10s per layer), and my image has many layers.

I'm unfortunately stuck using an older version of skopeo, since there are no binaries available, except for what is distributed via apt.

I understand that a newer version has the ability to upload layers concurrently, which would help a lot.

But why is this work performed at all? Aren't existing layers known in the remote manifest, to avoid even attempting blobs that already exist?

And is there anything I can do to overcome this issue? e.g. using a reverse proxy with caching? Are these GET/HEAD requests that can be cached?

```
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
Copying blob <redacted> skipped: already exists  
...
Copying blob <redacted> done  
Copying config <redacted> done  
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

The above really says _nothing at_ all about the source of the slowness. Is it DNS? Is it the link’s bandwidth? Latency? Is it the remote server? Is it the local disk? (What even are the source / destination transports?) Those are all things you will need to diagnose locally.

_If_ we are talking about a registry upload, we don’t check the remote manifest because users _typically_ upload something new, and the extra check would slow them down; whereas checking whether each individual layer already exists is ~required by the protocol and thus zero-cost. There is `c/image/copy.Options.OptimizeDestinationImageAlreadyExists`, which makes copies that change _nothing_ faster (those are typically extremely unlikely), at the cost of slowing down all other copies. That option is enabled by default in `skopeo sync` and not exposed as a user option in the CLI.

(Ultimately, if you are stuck on an old version, it’s not clear to me what improvement you expect to happen if you can’t update.)

Thanks for suggestions of diagnostic criteria. I've been struggling with this for a long time.

I tried replacing zot with distribution, s3 storage with local storage, and it performed the same, but after a number of other changes, it seems to work correctly now. Maybe it was latency related, but both iperf3 and mtr was giving good numbers (300 Mbps and 30ms). Thanks anyway!
(to clarify, the `skopeo copy` went from 3m20s to 5s, so you can imagine my relief)
